name: Paul Mockapetris
why_notable: Invented DNS. Wrote RFC 882 and RFC 883 (1983), defining the Domain Name System — the hierarchical, distributed database that replaced HOSTS.TXT. His design decisions: delegation, caching with TTLs, record types, UDP port 53. That architecture is essentially unchanged 40+ years later. Every dig, every nslookup, every subdomain enumeration run is operating on his spec.
